description: Danish writer and poet (1805–1875)
description_wiki: Hans Christian Andersen ( AN-dər-sən; Danish: [ˈhænˀs ˈkʰʁestjæn ˈɑnɐsn̩, - ˈkʰʁæs-] ; 2 April 1805 – 4 August 1875) was a Danish author.

description: county seat of Knox County, Indiana, United States
description_wiki: Vincennes is a city in and the county seat of Knox County, Indiana, United States.

description_wiki: Fort Sackville may refer to:

description: city in and county seat of Wayne County, and largest city in State of Michigan, United States
description_wiki: Detroit (  dih-TROYT, locally also  DEE-troyt) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Michigan.

description: city in and county seat of Rensselaer County, New York, United States
description_wiki: Troy is a city in and the county seat of Rensselaer County, New York, United States.

aliases: The Episcopal Church in the United States of America
Protestant Episcopal Church in the United States
Protestant Episcopal Church in the U.S.A.
description: American Anglican denomination (1789-)
description_wiki: The Episcopal Church (TEC), also known as the Protestant Episcopal Church in the United States of America (PECUSA), is a member of the worldwide Anglican Communion, based in the United States.

description: painting by Julian Hooper
former colony in North America, part of Great Britain
description_wiki: The Province of New York was a British proprietary colony and later a  royal colony on the northeast coast of North America from 1664 to 1783.

description: 17th-century Dutch colonial settlement that became New York City
description_wiki: New Amsterdam (Dutch: Nieuw Amsterdam, pronounced [ˌniu.ɑmstərˈdɑm]) was a 17th-century Dutch settlement established at the southern tip of Manhattan Island that served as the seat of the colonial government in New Netherland.

description: colonial army during the American Revolutionary War
description_wiki: The Continental Army was the army of the United Colonies representing the Thirteen Colonies and later the United States during the American Revolutionary War.

description: founding Father and first U.S. president; soldier and slave owner (1732–1799)
description_wiki: George Washington (February 22, 1732 [O.S. February 11, 1731] – December 14, 1799) was a Founding Father and the first president of the United States, serving from 1789 to 1797.

description: patriotic-hereditary society
description_wiki: The Society of the Cincinnati is a fraternal, hereditary society founded in 1783 to commemorate the American Revolutionary War that saw the creation of the United States.

description: period of ancient Roman civilization (509 BC–27 BC)
description_wiki: The Roman Republic (Latin: Res publica Romana, pronounced [ˈreːs ˈpuːblɪka roːˈmaːna]) was the era of classical Roman civilisation beginning with the overthrow of the Roman Kingdom (traditionally dated to 509 BC) and ending in 27 BC with the establishment of the Roman Empire following the War of Actium.

description: series of three wars fought between Rome and Carthage from 264 BC to 146 BC, at the time probably the largest wars that had ever taken place
description_wiki: The Punic Wars were a series of wars fought between the Roman Republic and the Carthaginian Empire during the period 264 to 146 BC. Three such wars took place, involving a total of forty-three years of warfare on both land and sea across the western Mediterranean region, and a four-year-long revolt against Carthage.

description: Founding Father, U.S. Chief Justice from 1789 to 1795
description_wiki: John Jay (December 23 [O.S. December 12], 1745 – May 17, 1829) was an American statesman, diplomat, signatory of the Treaty of Paris, and a Founding Father of the United States.

description: territory of the United States between 1787 and 1803
description_wiki: The Northwest Territory, also known as the Old Northwest and formally known as the Territory Northwest of the River Ohio, was formed from part of the unorganized western territory of the United States after the American Revolution.

description: village and former state capital of Illinois, in Randolph County west of the Mississippi
description_wiki: Kaskaskia is a village in Randolph County, Illinois, on the west side of the Mississippi River, although the former capital of the Illinois Territory was once on the river's eastern bank.

description: American writer, historian and diplomat (1783-1859)
description_wiki: Washington Irving (April 3, 1783 – November 28, 1859) was an American short-story writer, essayist, biographer, historian, and diplomat of the early 19th century.

description: German-American businessman
description_wiki: John Jacob Astor (born Johann Jakob Astor; July 17, 1763 – March 29, 1848) was a German-born American businessman, merchant, real estate mogul, and investor.

description: British shipowner and Conservative politician (1787–1845)
description_wiki: Joseph Somes (9 December 1787 – 25 June 1845) was a British shipowner and Conservative politician.

description: town and civil parish in South Oxfordshire, England
description_wiki: Wallingford () is a market town and civil parish on the River Thames in South Oxfordshire, England, 12 miles (19 km) north of Reading, 13 miles (21 km) south of Oxford and 11 miles (18 km) north west of Henley-on-Thames.

description: public university with its main campus in Vincennes, Indiana
description_wiki: Vincennes University (VU) is a public college with its main campus in Vincennes, Indiana, United States.

description: defunct department store chain
description_wiki: Gimbel Brothers, commonly known as Gimbels, was an American department store corporation that operated from 1842 until 1987.

description: city in and county seat of Anne Arundel County, Maryland, United States, and Maryland federated state capital city
description_wiki: Annapolis (  ə-NAP-əl-iss) is the capital of the U.S. state of Maryland.

description: international seaport located in New Orleans, Louisiana, United States
description_wiki: The Port of New Orleans is a significant transport hub located in Louisiana, United States.

description: city in Christian County, Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: Hopkinsville is a city in and the county seat of Christian County, Kentucky, United States.

description: city and county seat of Vigo County, Indiana, United States
description_wiki: Terre Haute ( TERR-ə HOHT) is a city in Vigo County, Indiana, United States, and its county seat.

aliases: State of Rhode Island and Providence Plantations
description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Rhode Island (  ROHD) is a state in the New England region of the Northeastern United States.

aliases: George III of the United Kingdom
George III, King of Great Britain
George III, Elector and King of Hanover
description: King of Great Britain and Ireland from 1760 to 1820 (1738–1820)
description_wiki: George III (George William Frederick; 4 June 1738 – 29 January 1820) was King of Great Britain and Ireland from 25 October 1760 until his death in 1820.

description: Governor of the state of Rhode Island (1731-1809)
description_wiki: William Greene Jr.

description: American politician from Kentucky (1777-1852)
description_wiki: Henry Clay (April 12, 1777 – June 29, 1852) was an American lawyer, statesman, and diplomat who represented Kentucky in both the U.S. Senate and House of Representatives.

description: American general in the American Revolutionary War (1742-1786)
description_wiki: Major General Nathanael Greene (August 7, 1742 – June 19, 1786) was an American military officer and planter who served in the Continental Army during the Revolutionary War.

description: town and the county seat of Kent County, Rhode Island, United States
description_wiki: East Greenwich is a town and the county seat of Kent County, Rhode Island.

description: non-profit organization in the USA
description_wiki: The Rhode Island Historical Society is a privately endowed membership organization, founded in 1822, dedicated to collecting, preserving, and sharing the history of Rhode Island.

entity: Eighteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution
aliases: 18th Amendment to the United States Constitution
description: amendment establishing Prohibition in the United States of America
description_wiki: The Eighteenth Amendment (Amendment XVIII) to the United States Constitution established the prohibition of alcohol in the United States.

entity: Nineteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution
aliases: 19th Amendment to the United States Constitution
description: 1920 amendment mandating women's suffrage
description_wiki: The Nineteenth Amendment (Amendment XIX) to the United States Constitution prohibits the United States and its states from denying the right to vote to citizens of the United States on the basis of sex, in effect recognizing the right of women to vote.

entity: United States Army Corps of Engineers
surface_forms: Army Department, Chief of the Engineering Department
description: direct reporting unit and U.S. Army's branch for military engineering
description_wiki: The United States Army Corps of Engineers (USACE) is the military engineering branch of the United States Army.

description: tributary of the Ohio River in the United States
description_wiki: The Wabash River  WAW-bash (French: Ouabache [wabaʃ]) is a  503-mile-long (810 km) river that drains most of the state of Indiana, and a significant part of Illinois, in the United States.

description: Illinois tributary of the Mississippi River in the United States
description_wiki: The Illinois River (Miami-Illinois: Inoka Siipiiwi) is a principal tributary of the Mississippi River at approximately 273 miles (439 km) in length.

aliases: Republican Party of the United States
description: American political party
description_wiki: The Republican Party, commonly known as the Grand Old Party (GOP), is the major conservative and right-wing political party in the United States.

description: rugby player
description_wiki: Louis Stevenson (31 January 1864 – 19 August 1931) is a former Scotland international rugby union player.

description: American politician and diplomat (1900–1965); 31st governor of Illinois from 1949 to 1953 (1900–1965)
description_wiki: Adlai Ewing Stevenson II (; February 5, 1900 – July 14, 1965) was an American politician and diplomat who was the United States ambassador to the United Nations from 1961 until his death in 1965.

description: American Class I railroad (1846-1968)
description_wiki: The Pennsylvania Railroad (reporting mark PRR), officially the Pennsylvania Railroad Company, also known as the "Pennsy," was an American Class I railroad established in 1846 and headquartered in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

description: town in Adams Township, Parke County, Indiana, United States
description_wiki: Rockville is a town in Adams Township, Parke County, in the U.S. state of Indiana.

description: Deakyne, Herbert; class of 1890
description_wiki: Herbert Deakyne (December 29, 1867 – May 28, 1945) was a United States Army officer and engineer in the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

description: president of the United States from 1929 to 1933 (1874–1964)
description_wiki: Herbert Clark Hoover (August 10, 1874 – October 20, 1964) was the 31st president of the United States, serving from 1929 to 1933.

description: head of the U.S. Department of Commerce
description_wiki: The United States secretary of commerce (SecCom) is the head of the United States Department of Commerce.

description: river in the midwestern United States
description_wiki: The Ohio River (Seneca: Ohi:yo') is a 981-mile-long (1,579 km) river in the United States.

description: city and county seat in Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Pittsburgh ( PITS-burg) is a city in Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, United States, and its county seat.

description: city in Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Cairo ( KAIR-oh, sometimes  KAY-roh) is the southernmost city in the U.S. state of Illinois and the county seat of Alexander County.
